metadata.title: FDA Webinar: Appropriate Use of Voluntary Consensus Standards in Premarket Submissions for Medical Devices — Final Guidance
metadata.note: This PDF contains the transcript of an FDA CDRH webinar discussing the final guidance on 'Appropriate Use of Voluntary Consensus Standards in Premarket Submissions for Medical Devices' (September 2018). The filename 38-dissolution-testing-ir-products.pdf does not match the document content.

rules.rule_text: When a submitter declares conformity to an FDA recognized standard, a Declaration of Conformity (DOC) must be included in the premarket submission.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the submitter declares Conformity to an FDA recognized standard, a Declaration of Conformity must be included in the premarket submission.
rules.exceptions: General use of a consensus standard (partial or without full conformity declaration) does not require a DOC.

rules.rule_text: FDA expects that all testing required by the consensus standard will be performed and conformance to the consensus standard will be met before premarket submission; a Declaration of Conformity cannot be submitted until testing is complete.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA expects that all testing required by the consensus standard will be performed and conformance to the consensus standard will be met before premarket submission. All normative requirements have been met and most testing conducted on a finished device or a final finished device.

rules.rule_text: Submitters may not use a Declaration of Conformity with a promissory statement; a Declaration of Conformity may only be submitted to a recognized standard if conformance has been met prior to the submission.
rules.verbatim_quote: Submitters may not use a Declaration of Conformity with a promissory statement. Submitters may only use a Declaration of Conformity to a recognized standard if conformance has been met prior to the submission.
rules.exceptions: Promissory statements may be used in certain situations such as installation requirements, chronic or long-term testing, or postmarket testing, but not within a Declaration of Conformity under 514(c).

rules.rule_text: Deviations may be made to a recognized standard but not within the context of a Declaration of Conformity; deviations must be addressed through general use documentation.
rules.verbatim_quote: We clarified that deviations may be made to a standard but not within the context of a Declaration of Conformity. This would be general use as described in the guidance.

rules.rule_text: A Declaration of Conformity must include the name and address of the applicant or sponsor responsible for the Declaration, the product or device identification, the Statement of Conformity, a list of standards to which the Declaration applies including the FDA recognition number for each standard, the date and place of issuance, signature and printed name and function of the responsible applicant, and any limitation on the validity of the Declaration.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Declaration of Conformity should include these elements: name and address of the applicant or sponsor responsible for the Declaration of Conformity; the product or device identification; the Statement of Conformity; a list of standards or which the Declaration of Conformity applies including, for each standard, and the options selected if any; the FDA recognition number for each standard; the date and place of issuance of the Declaration; signature, printed name, and function of the applicant or sponsor responsible for the Declaration of Conformity; and any limitation on the validity of the Declaration of Conformity.

rules.rule_text: A Declaration of Conformity may only be submitted to a standard that is currently recognized by FDA; submitters may not submit a Declaration of Conformity to a standard no longer recognized by FDA.
rules.verbatim_quote: the FDA recognition number for each standard. Remember, you can only submit a Declaration of Conformity to a recognized standard by FDA.

rules.rule_text: Falsification of a Declaration of Conformity submitted under section 514(c) of the FD&C Act is a prohibited act.
rules.verbatim_quote: We know Declaration of Conformity is an official statement and falsification of a Declaration of Conformity submitted under section 514(c) of the FD&C Act is a prohibited act.

rules.rule_text: Under Section 501(e)(2) of the FD&C Act, a device is adulterated if it is declared to be in conformity with a recognized consensus standard unless the device is in all respects in conformity with such standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: Under section 501(e)(2) of the FD&C Act, a device is adulterated if, among other conditions, it is declared to be in conformity with a recognized consensus standard unless the device is in all respects in conformity with such standard.

rules.rule_text: During review of a premarket submission containing a Declaration of Conformity, FDA will verify that the elements of ISO/IEC 17050-1 are present, the standards identified are recognized consensus standards, no deviations have been made to the normative requirements, the recognized standard is applicable to the device under review, and the Declaration does not include a promissory statement.
rules.verbatim_quote: During review of a premarket submission in which a Declaration of Conformity as been submitted, FDA will review the Declaration of Conformity and determine whether the following have been met: the elements in this guidance as well as ISO/IEC 17050-1 are present, the standard or standards identified in the Declaration of Conformity are recognized consensus standards, there have been no deviations made to the normative requirements of the FDA recognized standard identified, the FDA recognized standard in the Declaration of Conformity are or is applicable to the medical device under review, the supporting documentation - if determined to be necessary per ISO 17050-1 - is provided according to ISO 17050-2 or equivalent, the data or information submitted to support such Declaration demonstrates that the device is in Conformity with the normative requirements of the standard, the Declaration of Conformity does not include a promissory statement.

rules.rule_text: Supporting information must accompany a Declaration of Conformity when an FDA recognized consensus standard describes a test procedure but does not include performance limits or pass/fail criteria, or when the standard includes choices requiring submitter selection.
rules.verbatim_quote: Supporting information should accompany a Declaration of Conformity when: an FDR recognized consensus standard describes a test procedure but does not include performance limits or pass fail criteria. The submitter should provide an assessment of the results and how Conformity was determined. When the FDA recognized consensus standard includes choices related to for example, what is to be tested, which test methods to use, or the performance limits to test Conformity, or when it describes a process like risk assessment, the submitter should include an explanation for those choices and the selections made.
rules.exceptions: Supporting information is not required when the FDA-recognized consensus standard includes both a test method or procedure with predefined performance limits or acceptance criteria.

rules.rule_text: Complete test reports are required when a Declaration of Conformity is not provided, or when the standard has neither test methods nor predefined acceptance criteria, or when deviations have been made to the recognized standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: Complete test reports are requested when a Declaration of Conformity is not provided or the standard has neither test methods or predefined acceptance criteria or deviations have been made to the recognized standard. In any of these situations, FDA would expect to see a complete test report so that it could understand those adaptations or modifications to testing in the product or device.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturers must establish and maintain procedures for validating device design and monitoring and control of process parameters for validated processes to ensure that specified requirements continue to be met, in accordance with 21 CFR Part 820.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Quality system regulation in 21 CFR part 820, by which a manufacturer must also establish and maintain procedures for validating a device design and monitoring and control of process parameters for validated processes to ensure that the specified requirements continue to be met.

rules.rule_text: Recognition of a revised standard by FDA is not automatic; once a consensus standard is recognized, recognition of its revisions requires separate FDA evaluation and publication in the Federal Register.
rules.verbatim_quote: Similarly, once a consensus standard is recognized, recognition of its revisions is not automatic. In general, FDA actively assesses the impact of new consensus standards and revisions of existing standards on the premarket review process and recognizes these standards as appropriate. As new or revised standards are recognized by FDA, the recognition will be published in the Federal Register and listed on the CDRH Web site.

rules.rule_text: A superseded standard that FDA has withdrawn from recognition cannot be subsequently used toward a Declaration of Conformity for new devices citing the old standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: Superseded standards that FDA has withdrawn from the list of recognized standards cannot be subsequently used towards a Declaration of Conformity.
rules.exceptions: A cleared or approved device that received clearance based partly on a Declaration of Conformity to a since-withdrawn standard remains legally marketed; however, new 510(k) submissions citing it as a predicate cannot rely on the DOC to the no-longer-recognized standard.

rules.rule_text: During active review of a submission, if a consensus standard is updated or revised and recognized, FDA will generally continue to review the submission based on the previously recognized version unless the updated standard addresses a new safety or effectiveness issue relevant to the final decision.
rules.verbatim_quote: During review: Generally while a submission is undergoing active review and in the interim, a consensus standard is updated or revised and recognized, FDA will continue to review that submission based upon the previously recognized version.
rules.exceptions: If the updated or revised consensus standard addresses a new safety or effectiveness issue that is relevant to the final decision, and if that information is not described elsewhere in the submission, FDA may ask the submitter to either meet the portions of the new or updated standard that addresses that safety or effectiveness issue, or provide alternative data or information with scientific rationale.

rules.rule_text: Any interested party may submit a request for recognition of an appropriate standard established by a nationally or internationally recognized standard development organization, and FDA must respond within a 60-day timeframe under the 21st Century Cures Act.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any interested party may submit a request for recognition of an appropriate standard established by a nationally or internationally recognized standard development organization. Specific changes included: adding a 60-day timeframe for FDA's response to recognition requests.

rules.rule_text: FDA is required to publish on its website its rationale for recognition of all, part, or none of a standard and to issue a statement to the requester that includes the scientific, technical, regulatory, or other basis for the determination.
rules.verbatim_quote: directing FDA to publish on its website its rationale for recognition of all, part, or none of a standard that is subject of the request and issue a statement to the requester of its rationale for that determination. The statement will include the scientific, technical, regulatory, or other basis for the determination.

rules.rule_text: Non-clinical laboratory testing conducted to support a Declaration of Conformity must adhere to 21 CFR Part 58 Good Laboratory Practices to establish quality and reliability of test results.
rules.verbatim_quote: 21 CFR Part 58 Good Laboratory Practices establishes quality and reliability of how labs will conduct non-clinical laboratory testing.

rules.rule_text: Supplemental documentation supporting a Declaration of Conformity must describe the object under test, the test method or procedure used, whether Good Laboratory Practices or Quality System Regulations were followed, the results of testing and acceptance criteria, and an assessment of how normative requirements were met including rationales for any modifications.
rules.verbatim_quote: At a minimum, the following questions should be asked of the standard -- a description of the object under test and whether that is a final device, final finished device, or an attribute of the device; a description of the test method or procedure and whether Good Laboratory Practices or Quality System Regulations were followed; the results of the testing and acceptance criteria; and an assessment and results of how the normative requirements were met, including rationales for any selection, adaptations, modifications, or concessions that were made.

rules.rule_text: Testing for a Declaration of Conformity must be conducted on a finished device or final finished device, not on subcomponents or attributes in isolation where the full standard requires completed device testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: All normative requirements have been met and most testing conducted on a finished device or a final finished device.

rules.rule_text: When a manufacturer uses only part of a recognized standard (general use), the submitter must explain why certain sections were not applicable and provide the appropriate justification in the submission documentation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Certain areas may not be applicable, and in your Declaration what you may want to provide is the justification that would dictate why a certain section was not applicable and provide the appropriate justification to that as well. That would be the key in understanding why you didn't test to a certain subclause or normative requirement in the standard simply because the design or technological features based upon your device.

rules.rule_text: Normative references in an FDA recognized consensus standard are recognized by FDA only to the extent that they are used within the FDA recognized standard; such normative standards are not automatically recognized as independent entities.
rules.verbatim_quote: So does FDA recognition of a standard mean that all the normative references are automatically recognized? No. Normative references are recognized by FDA only to the extent that they are used within the FDA recognized standard. Such normative standards are not automatically recognized as independent entities unless it is felt that independently the use of those standards would also be useful to support the relevant parts of the Act under 514(c).

rules.rule_text: Transition periods apply when standards are withdrawn and replaced with newer versions; the duration of a transition period is specified in the Supplementary Information Sheet (SIS) and represents the period during which the outgoing standard remains recognized.
rules.verbatim_quote: We look at the impact of that standard and try to specify the amount of time to which the outgoing standard should remain, in effect, a recognized standard until it is officially withdrawn. That information is contained in the Supplementary Information Sheet or SIS below the extent of recognition.

rules.rule_text: Standards impacting large broad areas such as Quality Systems Regulations or major horizontal processes will typically receive a longer two-to-three year transition period when superseded by a newer recognized version.
rules.verbatim_quote: Standards that do impact large broad areas such as the Quality Systems Regulations or other major horizontal processes will typically receive a longer two to three year transition. We also do consider the ISO/IEC implementation withdrawal dates listed or if other standard developing organizations make recommendations.

rules.rule_text: FDA encourages early interaction with submitters to ensure scientific issues related to changing standards are addressed prior to the submission of a marketing application for a device.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA values and encourages early interaction with submitters to ensure scientific issues are addressed prior to the submission of a marketing application for a device, including the discussion of the changing standard.

rules.rule_text: The Supplementary Information Sheet (SIS) identifies sections or clauses in a standard that are excluded from FDA's recognition; submitters must be aware of these exclusions when preparing a Declaration of Conformity.
rules.verbatim_quote: The supplementary information sheet is CDRH's determination of how the recognized standard may be used in CDRH's regulatory activities. For example, a supplementary information sheet or SIS may describe the select the sections or clauses in the standard that are excluded from FDA's recognition.

rules.rule_text: General use of a consensus standard occurs when a submitter chooses to conform to a standard in part or in whole but does not submit a Declaration of Conformity; in this case the submitter must provide documentation explaining how and to what extent the standard was used.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'General use' of a consensus standard in premarket submissions is when a submitter chooses to conform to a consensus standard in part or in whole, but does not submit a Declaration of Conformity.

rules.rule_text: When different normative references may apply or when using a newer version of a normative reference than that cited in the parent standard, submitters must clearly outline the impact assessment and rationale in the submission documentation.
rules.verbatim_quote: When you are using different normative references or updated references, it will be very important for you to clearly outline why you did that so that it is clear to the review team when they are assessing the overall submission.

rules.rule_text: For FDA recognized consensus standards that have been updated or revised during the active review of a submission that address a new safety or effectiveness issue, FDA may ask the submitter to meet the relevant portions of the new standard or provide alternative data with scientific rationale.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the updated or revised consensus standard addresses a new safety or effectiveness issue that is relevant to the final decision of that submission, and if that information is not described elsewhere in the submission, FDA may ask the submitter to either meet the portions of the new or updated standard that addresses that safety or effectiveness issue, or provide alternative data or information along with scientific rationale for why the alternative addresses the issue.

rules.rule_text: Changes in a recognized consensus standard after clearance or approval is granted do not retroactively affect the product's clearance or approval status; a legacy device is not required to demonstrate to the agency that it has updated its file to a newer standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: After review, just as standards may be revised before submission they most certainly will be revised after clearance or approval is granted. Changes in the recognized consensus standard do not retroactively affect the product's clearance or approval status.
rules.exceptions: Quality System Regulations and/or quality system standards may ask for a process improvement or quality improvement where newer standards and methods are assessed to maintain the state of art activities supporting the quality of a device.
